article_meta.title: Polling analyst Patrick Allocco told Newsmax on Monday that despite wide disparities in early surveys on U.S

reasoning: The model repeatedly flagged quoted/attributed source language as author bias and used non-approved bias type labels. It caught one genuine author-level bias (promotional self-advertising) but mislabeled it. Overall, significant attribution violations and label inaccuracies.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Items 2-4 are direct quotes or clearly attributed to Allocco; item 2 is a paraphrased attribution. These should not be flagged as author bias.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most flagged items are not genuine author-prose bias due to attribution violations. Only the promotional block is a valid author-level bias instance.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The main missed area is the extent of the promotional/ad copy; additional unsubstantiated promotional claims could have been flagged, but the article otherwise contains little author prose beyond attribution.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: All five labels are outside the allowed set. Even the valid promotional bias should have been labeled with an allowed type (e.g., Bias by Story Choice and Placement or Slant/Unsubstantiated Claims).
